project.description: Ship a Docker-first, contributor-ready public GitHub project that you can already use in your own work.
project.name: Data Workbench

summary: Generated 26 work items from `plans/data-workbench/revisions/r006-approved-plan.md` using companion task input `plans/data-workbench/revisions/r006-approved-tasks.md`.

tasks.description:
This milestone covers `Phase 1 — Dogfood Baseline` in the shipping track.

### Why this milestone exists
Ship a Docker-first, contributor-ready public GitHub project that you can already use in your own work.

Make the repo understandable, runnable, and safe to contribute to without turning this release into a full public-product polish cycle.

### Phase estimate
- ``2-3 focused days``

### PM expectation
- use this phase to track release readiness, not only code output
- child tasks should make the public-share path understandable from Notion alone
tasks.title: Phase 1 — Dogfood Baseline

tasks.description:
This shipping task belongs to `Phase 1 — Dogfood Baseline`.

### Why this matters
make `docker compose up --build` the default happy path for a clean clone and remove avoidable startup ambiguity

### Release goal alignment
Ship a Docker-first, contributor-ready public GitHub project that you can already use in your own work.

Make the repo understandable, runnable, and safe to contribute to without turning this release into a full public-product polish cycle.

### Task goal
app, worker, and mirrored persistence come up predictably enough for daily use

### Validation
- clean-clone Docker smoke, `/healthz`, onboarding page load, graph load

### Parallelization
serial anchor for all other Docker and docs work.

### Done means
- the shipped behavior and public docs both reflect the task outcome
- the release plan can advance without hidden repo-only assumptions

### Tracking code
- `P1.1`
tasks.title: P1.1 Docker smoke path stabilization
